title: San Thome Basilica
tagline: San Thome Basilica — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: San Thome Church, officially known as the National Shrine of St.
overview.description: San Thome Church, officially known as the National Shrine of St. Thomas Cathedral Basilica, is a church of the Catholic Church in India, at the Santhome neighbourhood of Chennai, in Tamil Nadu. It is the cathedral of the Archdiocese of Madras-Mylapore, and a minor basilica dedicated to Saint Thomas the Apostle. The present structure dates back to 1523 AD, when it was built by the Portuguese over the tomb of Thomas.

topPlaces.name: Archdiocese of Madras and Mylapore
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Madras and Mylapore (Latin: Madraspolitan(us) et Meliaporen(sis)) is an archdiocese based in the city of Madras (now Chennai), in India. It merged with the ancient diocese of Mylapore which now a part of Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Consulate General of Russia, Chennai
topPlaces.description: The Consulate General of the Russian Federation in Chennai (Russian: Генеральное консульство России в Ченнаи) represents the interests of the Russian government in the southern region of India. The other missions are the Embassy of the Russian Federation in New Delhi and the Consulate General of the Russian Federation in Kolkata and Mumbai.

topPlaces.name: Santhome
topPlaces.description: Santhome, also written San Thome, is a locality in Mylapore in Chennai city (formerly Madras) in India. It was a Portuguese settlement in the 16th century.

topPlaces.name: Foreshore Estate
topPlaces.description: Foreshore Estate (Tamil: பட்டினப்பாக்கம் Pattinapakkam) is a neighbourhood in Chennai, India. It is situated along the southern stretch of the Marina Beach.

topPlaces.name: Kutchery Road metro station
topPlaces.description: Kutchery Road is an upcoming underground metro station on the East-West Corridor of the Yellow Line of Chennai Metro in Chennai, India. This metro station will be among the 30 stations of Corridor IV and 12 underground stations along Corridor IV of the Chennai Metro, Poonamallee Bypass–Lighthouse stretch.

topPlaces.name: Virupakshiswarar Temple, Mylapore
topPlaces.description: Virupakshiswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mylapore in Chennai, India. It is dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Malleeswarar Temple, Mylapore
topPlaces.description: Malleeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mylapore in Chennai, India. It is dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Madhava Perumal temple, Mylapore
topPlaces.description: The Madhava Perumal Temple is dedicated to Hindu deity Vishnu, located in Mylapore, Chennai, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Madhava Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Amirtavalli.

items.note: The Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Madras and Mylapore (Latin: Madraspolitan(us) et Meliaporen(sis)) is an archdiocese based in the city of Madras (now Chennai), in India. It merged with the ancient diocese of Mylapore which now a part of Chennai.

The other missions are the Embassy of the Russian Federation in New Delhi and the Consulate General of the Russian Federation in Kolkata and Mumbai.

Foreshore Estate (Tamil: பட்டினப்பாக்கம் Pattinapakkam) is a neighbourhood in Chennai, India. It is situated along the southern stretch of the Marina Beach.

Kutchery Road is an upcoming underground metro station on the East-West Corridor of the Yellow Line of Chennai Metro in Chennai, India. This metro station will be among the 30 stations of Corridor IV and 12 underground stations along Corridor IV of the Chennai Metro, Poonamallee Bypass–Lighthouse stretch.

Malleeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mylapore in Chennai, India. It is dedicated to Shiva.

The Madhava Perumal Temple is dedicated to Hindu deity Vishnu, located in Mylapore, Chennai, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Madhava Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Amirtavalli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit San Thome Basilica?
faq.a: The best time to visit San Thome Basilica is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach San Thome Basilica?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (12 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for San Thome Basilica?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of San Thome Basilica at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in San Thome Basilica?
faq.a: Stays in San Thome Basilica start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in San Thome Basilica?
faq.a: The top attractions are Archdiocese of Madras and Mylapore, Consulate General of Russia, Chennai, Santhome, Foreshore Estate, Kutchery Road metro station.
